data.title: Key Fed Inflation Rate Makes Rate Cut A Lock; S&P 500 Rallies
data.description: The current inflation rate is lower than the Federal Reserve's predicted level. This decrease in inflation has solidified expectations for a rate cut by the Fed. Following this news, the S&P 500 experienced a surge in performance.

data.title: ADP Jobs Report Shows Surprise Drop In Private Payrolls; S&P 500 Wavers
data.description: The latest official jobs report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics is awaited until after the Federal Reserve meeting. Following the release of the ADP Jobs Report, there was an unexpected decline in private payrolls. This news caused fluctuations in the S&P 500 index.
Stocks Rally Toward Record Highs, Oil Crashes 7% As Iran Talks Show Progress: What's Moving Markets Tuesday?

data.title: S&P 500 Just Erased Seven Weeks Of War Losses, Nasdaq 100 Heads For 10th Consecutive Gain
data.description: The S&P 500 index has successfully regained all points lost since the onset of the conflict in Iran. Surging above the 6,900 mark on Tuesday, it exceeded the closing level on Feb. 27, effectively nullifying seven weeks of declines triggered by the war. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) is currently up by 0.40% at 6,913, poised for its ninth positive session out of the last 10. This rebound comes after the index plummeted from a peak of over 6,950 in late January to a low near 6,350 due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, leading to a spike in oil prices above $100 and causing a shift in inflation, growth, and Federal Reserve expectations simultaneously. Within a brief span, the index has recuperated from the seven consecutive weeks of losses in just ten sessions. Concurrently, the Nasdaq 100 is on track for its longest winning streak since 2021, setting a new record with ten consecutive gains. Market analyst David Morrison noted that various indices including the S&P 500, Nasdaq, Russell 2000, and Dow are now reflecting... (Continued on Benzinga.com)

data.title: AMD Rips Higher As TSMC's Blowout Guide Lights Up The Stock
data.description: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ: AMD) experienced a significant increase in value on Thursday following the impressive quarterly results and raised guidance from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (NYSE: TSM). TSMC, AMD's foundry partner for various products including MI350 and upcoming MI450 accelerators, as well as next-generation EPYC server CPUs, reported record profits for the fourth consecutive quarter. The company raised its 2026 revenue growth forecast to over 30%, attributing this to high demand for AI infrastructure and limited 3-nanometer capacity. TSMC's CEO C.C. Wei expressed confidence in a sustained AI growth trend, noting strong demand and positive customer feedback during the earnings call. The company foresees a multi-year cycle of AI expansion driving further growth. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Republicans Launch Tax Cut Ad Campaign In 14 Swing Districts, Betting 'No Tax On Tips' Wins Back Working-Class Voters
data.description: On April 14, the National Republican Congressional Committee initiated a digital ad campaign in 14 competitive congressional districts across 11 states. These ads promote GOP-led tax breaks under the 2025 tax and spending bill. The campaign focuses on benefits such as tax-free tips and overtime, positioning them as direct aids for working families. NRCC representative Mike Marinella stated, "House Republicans provided tangible relief after Democrats burdened working Americans for years." The campaign's cost was labeled as a "modest ad buy." President Trump hailed the bill's measures as "the largest tax cuts in American history." Despite a tight Senate approval, the legislation passed. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: US Targets Lending Reforms As Housing Shortfall Tops 10 Million
data.description: A recent report from the White House's Council of Economic Advisers revealed that the United States is currently experiencing a shortage of at least 10 million single-family homes. The shortage is a result of a significant slowdown in homebuilding that began after the 2008 financial crisis. According to the report, if construction had continued at pre-2008 rates, the US housing stock would be much larger today. The Council of Economic Advisers emphasized that this lack of housing is not due to temporary fluctuations in demand but rather to long-standing regulatory constraints, permitting delays, and increasing construction costs in various states. These constraints include zoning restrictions, delays in obtaining permits, expansions of building codes, and higher compliance costs. The report highlights the impact of these regulatory challenges on the residential construction sector.

data.title: Pearl Meyer Strengthens Executive Compensation Capabilities with Two New Managing Directors
data.description: Two accomplished professionals, Steve Charlebois and Sarah Holmes, have joined the executive compensation consulting and leadership advisory firm Pearl Meyer in Boston on April 16, 2026. With extensive experience in technology, health care, and the not-for-profit sectors, Charlebois and Holmes bring valuable insights to the team. This addition further enhances the firm's capabilities in providing top-notch services.

data.description: Over 60 days following a decision by a federal judge in New York permitting prosecutors to obtain AI conversations, numerous prominent law firms have issued cautions to clients.

data.title: Comcast Business Unveils Innovation Lab, Collaborating with Global Technology Leaders to Scale Next-Gen Enterprise Solutions
data.description: Comcast Business, a rapidly expanding enterprise technology provider, unveiled the Comcast Business Innovation Lab in Philadelphia. This specialized facility serves as a collaborative space for Comcast Business, its clients, and top tech partners to jointly create, assess, and expand enterprise solutions. The Lab is introduced with three initial program areas established in partnership with Dell Technologies, Digital Realty, and Expedient, and was announced at the 2026 Comcast event.

data.title: CareDx Sharpens Focus On Core Testing Services
data.description: In a major move, CareDx Inc. (NASDAQ: CDNA) has decided to sell its Lab Products business to EuroBio Scientific for a substantial amount of $170 million in cash. This decision signals a strategic shift towards the company's higher-growth precision testing and digital solutions segments. The transaction has been approved by the boards of both companies and is anticipated to be finalized by the conclusion of the third quarter of 2026. CareDx's stock is currently encountering significant resistance, prompting speculations about the reasons behind CDNA stock's sudden surge. CareDx's President and CEO, John Hanna, emphasized that this divestiture is in line with the company's strategic focus on its core Testing Services and Patient and Digital Solutions divisions. This decision comes following a robust performance in the first quarter, with Testing Services revenue witnessing a noteworthy 48% increase, and Patient and Digital Solutions registering a 33% growth. Hanna attributed this success to the company's effective "solutions-selling strategy." This development signifies a significant transition for CareDx towards its primary business segments.

data.title: Home Energy Rebates Are Coming: DOE Confirms Plans to Release Billions in Funds to States
data.description: The Building Performance Association commends the U.S. Department of Energy for allocating billions of dollars in State Home Energy Rebate Funds. This announcement was made in the administration's initial public address regarding the distribution of funds, on April 16, 2026 in Washington.

data.title: Ribbon-Cutting Ceremony Marks Official Opening of the Full Sail University IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range
data.description: In Winter Park, Florida, on April 16, 2026, Full Sail University unveiled the "Full Sail University IBM Cyber Defense Range powered by AWS and Cloud Range" after a ribbon-cutting event at the Winter Park campus. This new facility is equipped with cutting-edge technology and training resources to enhance cybersecurity education.

data.title: Atlas One Capital Corporation Announces Adoption of Semi-Annual Reporting
data.description: On April 16, 2026, in Toronto, Ontario, Atlas One Capital Corporation (TSXV: ACAP.P), a capital pool company on the TSX Venture Exchange, disclosed its plans to adhere to the guidelines in the Semi-Annual Reporting Pilot Program. This decision utilizes the exceptions offered through Coordinated Blanket Order 51-933, which allows for Semi-Annual Reporting for specified Venture Issuers.
